Most developers receive the normal plan review availability. > > For paid Pro and Pro+ PR reviews, CodeRabbit uses adaptive limits for sustained high-volume activity. When a developer's recent PR review activity reaches the 95th percentile or higher among CodeRabbit users, additional reviews become available more gradually as earlier reviews age out of the rolling window. > > Please refer [docs](https://docs.coderabbit.ai/management/plans#rate-limits) for additional details. > >

--- Comment `@coderabbitai help` to get the list of available commands.

## Code Review

This pull request introduces customizable keyboard layouts for the terminal special-keys bar, allowing users to define, edit, and duplicate layouts with one or two rows of custom keys, symbols, and modifier combos. It adds a database table and repository for layout persistence, a visual layout editor, and settings to customize key sizes and per-host keyboard suggestions. The reviewer feedback highlights several improvement opportunities, including validating layout names during rename to prevent database constraint crashes, safely parsing JSON null values, removing redundant list mappings, localizing hardcoded English strings for internationalization, and using lifecycle-aware flow collection in Compose.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> The [consumer version of Gemini Code Assist on GitHub](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/review-repo-code) is being sunset. Starting **June 18, 2026**, new organization installations will be blocked, and all code review activity will officially cease on **July 17, 2026**.
> For more details on the timeline and next steps, please review the [Help Documentation](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/deprecations/consumer-code-review).

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

Using `obj.has(KEY_LABEL) && obj.optString(KEY_LABEL)` can lead to a bug where the literal string `"null"` is returned if the JSON value is explicitly `null` (e.g., `{"label": null}`). This is due to Android's `JSONObject` coercion behavior where `JSONObject.NULL.toString()` returns `"null"`.

Using `obj.isNull(KEY_LABEL)` is the standard and safer way to handle both missing keys and explicit `null` values in Android's `org.json` library.

```suggestion
        val label = if (obj.isNull(KEY_LABEL)) null else obj.optString(KEY_LABEL)
        val icon = if (obj.isNull(KEY_ICON)) null else obj.optString(KEY_ICON)
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The expression `uiState.rows.map { it.ifEmpty { listOf() } }.map { it }` is redundant. Since `uiState.rows` is already a `List<List<KeySpec>>` and we have already guarded against it being empty, we can simplify this directly to `uiState.rows`.

```suggestion
                    layout = KeyboardLayoutSpec(uiState.rows),
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The mapping `this.map { it }` is redundant when converting the list to a mutable list. You can simplify this to `this.toMutableList()`.

```suggestion
    private fun List<List<KeySpec>>.toMutableRows(): MutableList<List<KeySpec>> = this.toMutableList()
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The strings `"New layout"` and `"copy"` are user-facing UI labels but are hardcoded in English. To support internationalization (i18n), these should be defined as string resources in `strings.xml` and retrieved dynamically using `context.getString(...)`.

You can inject `@ApplicationContext private val context: Context` into the `KeyboardLayoutsViewModel` constructor to access resources.

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

When collecting flows in Compose UI, it is recommended to use `collectAsStateWithLifecycle` instead of `collectAsState` to avoid wasting resources when the app is in the background.

```suggestion
    return flow.collectAsStateWithLifecycle(initial = DefaultKeyboardLayouts.default).value
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

Using `obj.has(KEY_LABEL) && obj.optString(KEY_LABEL)` can lead to a bug where the literal string `"null"` is returned if the JSON value is explicitly `null` (e.g., `{"label": null}`). This is due to Android's `JSONObject` coercion behavior where `JSONObject.NULL.toString()` returns `"null"`.

Using `obj.isNull(KEY_LABEL)` is the standard and safer way to handle both missing keys and explicit `null` values in Android's `org.json` library.

```suggestion
        val label = if (obj.isNull(KEY_LABEL)) null else obj.optString(KEY_LABEL)
        val icon = if (obj.isNull(KEY_ICON)) null else obj.optString(KEY_ICON)
```

```suggestion

```

```suggestion
    private fun List<List<KeySpec>>.toMutableRows(): MutableList<List<KeySpec>> = this.toMutableList()
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The strings `"New layout"` and `"copy"` are user-facing UI labels but are hardcoded in English. To support internationalization (i18n), these should be defined as string resources in `strings.xml` and retrieved dynamically using `context.getString(...)`.

You can inject `@ApplicationContext private val context: Context` into the `KeyboardLayoutsViewModel` constructor to access resources.

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

When collecting flows in Compose UI, it is recommended to use `collectAsStateWithLifecycle` instead of `collectAsState` to avoid wasting resources when the app is in the background.

